title: Solar Panel Installation Cost in Noida & Gurugram (2026 Complete Guide)
excerpt: Complete breakdown of solar panel installation costs in Noida and Gurugram — including PM Surya Ghar subsidy, ROI calculator, residential vs commercial pricing, and real customer examples.
author: Volta Solar Expert Team

inlineImages.name: Rooftop Solar Installation Noida
inlineImages.caption: A 5 kW on-grid rooftop solar system installed by Volta Solar in Sector 62, Noida — generating approx. 600 units/month.

inlineImages.name: PM Surya Ghar Subsidy Benefit
inlineImages.caption: PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana subsidy can reduce your solar installation cost by up to ₹78,000 for a 3 kW system.

inlineImages.name: Commercial Solar Plant Gurugram
inlineImages.caption: 50 kW commercial rooftop solar system at an industrial facility in Manesar, Gurugram — saving ₹4.2 lakh annually.

content:
If you've been thinking about going solar in Noida or Gurugram, the first question on your mind is probably: 'Kitna lagega?' (How much will it cost?)

Honest answer — it depends. But in this guide, we're going to break it down so clearly that by the end, you'll know exactly what a solar system costs in your city, what government subsidies you can claim, and how fast you'll recover your investment.

This is the most complete solar cost guide for Noida, Gurugram, and Uttar Pradesh in 2026. Let's get into it.

## Why 2026 Is the Best Year to Go Solar in UP & NCR

Electricity prices in Uttar Pradesh and Haryana have increased by 8–12% year-on-year for the past 5 years. Your electricity bill is only going to get higher.

Meanwhile, solar panel prices have dropped by over 60% in the last decade. The government is actively pushing rooftop solar through PM Surya Ghar — the biggest solar subsidy program India has ever seen.

Result? The payback period on a solar system in Noida today is just 3–4 years. After that, you're generating electricity for virtually nothing — for the next 21+ years.

[IMAGE:0]

## Solar Panel Installation Cost in Noida — 2026 Pricing Table

Here's the complete, transparent pricing for grid-connected rooftop solar systems in Noida, Greater Noida, and Ghaziabad:

| System Size | Suitable For | Gross Cost | PM Surya Ghar Subsidy | Net Cost After Subsidy |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 kW | 1 BHK / Low usage | ₹1,40,000 | ₹60,000 | ₹80,000 |
| 3 kW | 2–3 BHK Home | ₹1,95,000 | ₹78,000 | ₹1,17,000 |
| 4 kW | 3 BHK / Small Villa | ₹2,40,000 | ₹78,000 | ₹1,62,000 |
| 5 kW | Large Home / Duplex | ₹3,00,000 | ₹78,000 | ₹2,22,000 |
| 10 kW | Small Office / Shop | ₹5,50,000 | No subsidy | ₹5,50,000 |

### What Is Included in This Price?

When Volta Solar quotes you a price, here is exactly what you're getting:

- Tier-1 solar panels (Jinko Solar, Waaree, or Adani — 25-year output warranty)
- String or hybrid inverter with 5-year warranty
- Hot-dip galvanized MS mounting structure
- DC & AC wiring, junction boxes, earthing
- UPPCL / DVVNL net-metering application and approval
- Installation by certified electricians
- Commissioning and system handover
- Free AMC for the first year

No hidden charges. No surprise bills after installation.

## Solar Panel Installation Cost in Gurugram — 2026 Pricing

Gurugram falls under DHBVN (Dakshin Haryana Bijli Vitran Nigam). Solar pricing here is slightly different because permit timelines and net-metering processes follow Haryana's rules.

| System Size | Suitable For | Gross Cost | Subsidy | Net Cost |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 kW | Apartment / 1 BHK | ₹1,45,000 | ₹60,000 | ₹85,000 |
| 3 kW | 2–3 BHK Home | ₹2,00,000 | ₹78,000 | ₹1,22,000 |
| 5 kW | Villa / Large Home | ₹3,10,000 | ₹78,000 | ₹2,32,000 |
| 10 kW | Boutique Office | ₹5,80,000 | No subsidy | ₹5,80,000 |

[IMAGE:1]

## PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana — Subsidy Details 2026

This is the central government scheme that's making solar affordable for millions of Indian families. Here's how the subsidy works:

### How Much Subsidy Can You Get?

- First 2 kW: ₹30,000 per kW = ₹60,000
- 3rd kW: ₹18,000
- Maximum subsidy cap: ₹78,000 (for 3 kW system)
- For systems above 3 kW: Subsidy stays at ₹78,000 (does not increase further)

### Who Is Eligible?

- Residential consumers with a valid electricity connection
- Must own the property (or have landlord's consent)
- Must install through a MNRE-empaneled installer
- One subsidy per electricity meter

## Real-Life Example — Family in Sector 44, Noida

Let's look at a real customer example to make this concrete.

4-member family in a 3 BHK apartment, Sector 44, Noida. Monthly electricity bill: ₹5,500–₹6,000. Annual electricity cost: ~₹70,000.

System Installed: 4 kW on-grid solar by Volta Solar.

- Gross system cost: ₹2,40,000
- PM Surya Ghar subsidy: ₹78,000
- Net out-of-pocket cost: ₹1,62,000
- Monthly Generation: ~480 units
- Monthly Savings: ₹4,800–₹5,500
- Annual Savings: ~₹62,000–₹66,000
- Payback Period: 2.5 to 3 years

After payback, this family saves approximately ₹60,000+ every year — for the next 22 years. Total lifetime savings: over ₹13 lakh.

[IMAGE:2]

## Residential vs Commercial Solar — Cost Comparison

### Residential Solar (2 kW – 10 kW)

For homes in Noida, Greater Noida, Ghaziabad, and Gurugram, residential solar is the sweet spot. Subsidies are available, payback periods are short, and net metering makes the economics excellent.

- Best for: Independent houses, builder floors, villas, plotted housing
- Ideal size: 3 kW – 6 kW for most families
- Subsidy: Available up to ₹78,000
- Typical ROI: 3–5 years

### Commercial Solar (10 kW – 1 MW+)

For factories, warehouses, offices, schools, and hospitals across UP and Haryana, commercial solar makes even more sense — because your electricity consumption is higher and the savings are proportionally larger.

- Best for: Manufacturing units, IT offices, educational institutions, hospitals
- Ideal size: 50 kW – 500 kW for mid-sized businesses
- Tax benefit: 40% accelerated depreciation
- Typical ROI: 4–6 years

## 5 Expert Tips Before You Go Solar in Noida or Gurugram

- Always get a free site survey first. Don't trust quotes given over the phone without a rooftop inspection.
- Insist on Tier-1 panels. Brands like Jinko, Waaree, LONGi, and Adani have independently verified 25-year degradation rates.
- Check if your installer is MNRE-empaneled. Only registered installers can process PM Surya Ghar subsidies.
- Don't overlook net metering. Make sure your installer handles the DISCOM net-metering application.
- Ask about AMC. A good installer offers Annual Maintenance Contract covering cleaning, inverter checks, and performance reports.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What is the average solar panel installation cost in Noida in 2026?

A 3 kW on-grid residential solar system in Noida costs approximately ₹1,95,000 before subsidy. After the PM Surya Ghar subsidy of ₹78,000, the effective cost comes down to around ₹1,17,000.

### How much does solar installation cost in Gurugram?

Solar installation in Gurugram costs between ₹1,45,000 for a 2 kW system and ₹5,80,000 for a 10 kW commercial system. Residential systems between 2–5 kW qualify for PM Surya Ghar central subsidy up to ₹78,000.

### How long does it take for solar to pay for itself in UP?

For most homes in Uttar Pradesh and NCR, the payback period is 3–4 years after the PM Surya Ghar subsidy. After payback, the system generates electricity for free for 20+ more years.

### Is PM Surya Ghar subsidy available in Gurugram?

Yes. PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana is a central government scheme available across India, including Haryana. Gurugram residents with residential electricity connections can claim up to ₹78,000 in subsidy.

### What is the best solar system size for a 3 BHK home in Noida?

For a typical 3 BHK home in Noida consuming 250–350 units per month, a 3 kW to 4 kW on-grid solar system is ideal. A 3 kW system generates approximately 360–400 units/month.

## Ready to Go Solar? Here's Your Next Step

You've done the reading. You know the costs, the subsidies, and the ROI. Now it's time to find out exactly what a solar system will cost for YOUR home or business — with a free, no-obligation site survey by our expert team.

Volta Solar is UPPCL-approved, MNRE-empaneled, and has completed 500+ installations across Noida, Greater Noida, Ghaziabad, and Gurugram. We handle everything — from design and permits to subsidy paperwork and net-metering approval.

📞 Call us now: +91 93359 35751

No pressure. No hidden charges. Just honest solar advice from people who've done this 500+ times.

title: PM Surya Ghar Subsidy 2026: How to Apply Online in UP & Haryana (Step-by-Step)
cardTitle: PM Surya Ghar Subsidy: Complete Apply Guide
excerpt: Step-by-step guide to claiming PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana subsidy in Uttar Pradesh and Haryana — eligibility, documents needed, portal registration, and how Volta Solar handles it all for you.
author: Volta Solar Expert Team

inlineImages.name: Solar subsidy application portal
inlineImages.caption: The PM Surya Ghar portal (pmsuryaghar.gov.in) is the official gateway to claim your solar subsidy — Volta Solar manages this for all our customers.

inlineImages.name: Net meter installation UP
inlineImages.caption: Net meter installation by UPPCL is the final step before your subsidy is disbursed to your bank account.

content:
You've probably seen the ads. 'Get ₹78,000 from the government for going solar.' But when you actually try to figure out how to apply, it feels overwhelming — portals, documents, DISCOMs, consumer numbers.

Don't worry. By the end of this guide, you'll know exactly what PM Surya Ghar is, who qualifies, what documents you need, and how the money actually reaches your bank account. If you install with Volta Solar, we handle every single step for you.

## What Is PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana?

Launched in February 2024 by the Government of India, PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana is the country's largest residential rooftop solar mission. The target is to bring solar energy to 1 crore Indian households.

The scheme offers two key benefits:

- Direct subsidy of up to ₹78,000 on installation cost (transferred directly to your bank account)
- Up to 300 units of free electricity every month through net metering credits

It's not a loan. It's not a discount voucher. The government transfers actual cash to your bank after installation.

## How Much Subsidy Do You Get?

The subsidy is calculated on the basis of system size:

| System Size | Subsidy Per kW | Total Subsidy |
|---|---|---|
| 1 kW | ₹30,000/kW | ₹30,000 |
| 2 kW | ₹30,000/kW | ₹60,000 |
| 3 kW | ₹30,000 for first 2 kW + ₹18,000 for 3rd kW | ₹78,000 |
| Above 3 kW | Subsidy capped at ₹78,000 | ₹78,000 (max) |

So whether you install a 3 kW or a 10 kW system, the maximum subsidy is ₹78,000.

[IMAGE:0]

## Who Is Eligible for PM Surya Ghar Subsidy?

Eligibility is straightforward. You qualify if:

- You are an Indian resident with a valid residential electricity connection
- The property is in your name or you have the owner's written consent
- You have not previously availed any government solar subsidy on this connection
- Your installer is MNRE-empaneled (Volta Solar is registered)
- The system is on-grid (not off-grid or standalone)

One subsidy is allowed per electricity meter number. Commercial connections and industrial connections are NOT eligible for this scheme.

## Documents Required for PM Surya Ghar Application

Gather these before you begin:

- Aadhaar card (mandatory)
- Electricity bill (latest — showing your consumer number)
- Bank account details (passbook or cancelled cheque — for subsidy transfer)
- Passport-size photograph
- Property ownership proof (if required by your DISCOM)

Volta Solar collects these from you at the time of signing the installation agreement. You don't have to visit any office.

## Step-by-Step Application Process

### Step 1 — Register on the PM Surya Ghar Portal

Go to pmsuryaghar.gov.in. Click 'Apply for Rooftop Solar'. Enter your state, electricity distribution company (DISCOM), and consumer number. Register with your mobile number linked to Aadhaar.

### Step 2 — Apply for Feasibility Approval

After registration, submit an application to your DISCOM (UPPCL, DVVNL, DHBVN, etc.) for feasibility approval. This is essentially asking the grid authority if your connection is suitable for solar. Approval typically takes 7–15 working days.

### Step 3 — Install the Solar System

Once feasibility is approved, your MNRE-empaneled installer (that's us) installs the system. This takes 2–5 working days for a residential rooftop.

### Step 4 — Apply for Net Meter

After installation, submit a net meter application to your DISCOM through the portal. The DISCOM sends a team to inspect and install the net meter. This step typically takes 15–30 days.

### Step 5 — Submit Commissioning Certificate

Once the net meter is installed, your installer submits a commissioning certificate. Volta Solar handles this documentation entirely.

### Step 6 — Subsidy Is Credited to Your Bank

After verification, the subsidy amount (up to ₹78,000) is transferred directly to your bank account. Timeline: typically 30–60 days after commissioning.

[IMAGE:1]

## PM Surya Ghar in Uttar Pradesh — UPPCL Process

In UP, the application goes through UPPCL or your local DISCOM — DVVNL (Dakshin), PVVNL (Paschim), MVVNL (Madhyanchal), or PuVVNL (Poorvanchal). The process is the same but timelines may vary slightly by district.

Noida, Greater Noida, Ghaziabad — PVVNL
Lucknow, Kanpur — MVVNL
Agra, Mathura — DVVNL

Volta Solar has completed subsidy applications across all four UPDISCOMs. We know the local documentation quirks and follow up on your behalf until the money hits your account.

## PM Surya Ghar in Gurugram & Haryana — DHBVN Process

In Gurugram, the DISCOM is DHBVN (Dakshin Haryana Bijli Vitran Nigam). Haryana residents follow the same national portal process but DHBVN has its own internal timelines.

Haryana also runs state-level top-up incentives for solar in addition to the central subsidy. Contact us to check what's currently available in your district.

## Real Customer Story — Lucknow Family Saves ₹65,000 Per Year

Mr. Rakesh Sharma, a retired government employee in Gomti Nagar, Lucknow, installed a 3 kW system with Volta Solar in August 2025.

- Monthly bill before solar: ₹4,800
- System cost: ₹1,95,000
- PM Surya Ghar subsidy received: ₹78,000
- Net cost paid: ₹1,17,000
- Monthly savings now: ₹4,200–₹4,600
- Payback period: Under 2.5 years

'Maine socha tha bahut complicated hoga — forms, offices, chakkar. Volta Solar ne sab kuch kar diya. Sirf documents dene the aur subsidy bank mein aa gayi.' — Mr. Rakesh Sharma, Lucknow

## Common Mistakes to Avoid

- Installing with a non-MNRE-empaneled contractor — you will NOT get the subsidy
- Applying for an off-grid system — subsidy only applies to on-grid grid-tied systems
- Incomplete bank details — subsidy transfer fails and you have to reapply
- Not following up on net meter application — DISCOM delays are common; your installer should track this
- Applying without a site survey — wrong system size means you miss optimization and may not get full benefit

## Frequently Asked Questions

### How long does PM Surya Ghar subsidy take to arrive in UP?

After the net meter is installed and commissioning certificate submitted, subsidy typically arrives within 30–60 days. In UP's major cities like Noida and Lucknow, Volta Solar customers have received subsidy in as little as 28 days.

### Can I apply for PM Surya Ghar subsidy myself without an installer?

You can register on the portal yourself, but the installation must be done by an MNRE-empaneled installer to qualify for subsidy. Most customers let their installer handle the full process — it's faster and error-free.

### Is PM Surya Ghar subsidy taxable?

No. The PM Surya Ghar subsidy is a direct benefit transfer and is not treated as taxable income under current Income Tax rules. However, always consult your CA for your personal situation.

### Can tenants apply for PM Surya Ghar subsidy?

Yes, but the electricity connection and subsidy application must be in the name of the account holder. If you're a tenant, your landlord must give written consent, and the connection should be in your name.

### What happens if my DISCOM rejects the net meter application?

Rejections are rare but do happen due to paperwork issues. Volta Solar resubmits and follows up with the DISCOM directly. We have dedicated liaison officers for UPPCL, DVVNL, and DHBVN.

## Let Volta Solar Handle Everything

From portal registration to subsidy disbursal, Volta Solar manages the entire PM Surya Ghar application process for every customer in Noida, Gurugram, Ghaziabad, and across UP.

You focus on saving electricity. We handle the paperwork.

📞 Call us: +91 93359 35751

Book your free site survey today. We'll assess your roof, design the right system, and start your subsidy application on Day 1 of installation.

title: On-Grid vs Off-Grid vs Hybrid Solar System — Which is Best for Homes in Noida & UP?
cardTitle: On-Grid vs Off-Grid vs Hybrid: Which Solar?
excerpt: Can't decide between on-grid, off-grid, and hybrid solar systems? This plain-English guide explains the real differences, costs, and which system makes the most sense for Noida, Gurugram, and UP homes in 2026.
author: Volta Solar Expert Team

inlineImages.name: On-grid solar system inverter
inlineImages.caption: An on-grid solar inverter synchronizes your solar output with the utility grid — the most common and cost-effective setup for urban homes in NCR.

inlineImages.name: Hybrid solar system with battery
inlineImages.caption: A hybrid solar system with lithium battery storage — ideal for areas with frequent power cuts in UP and rural Haryana.

content:
When you decide to go solar, the first question your installer will ask is: 'On-grid chahiye, off-grid, ya hybrid?'

Most homeowners have no idea what any of this means. And some installers — frankly — take advantage of that confusion to sell you a more expensive system than you need.

This guide will give you a clear, jargon-free explanation of all three types so you can make a confident decision.

## The Core Difference — In One Line Each

- On-grid: Your solar system is connected to the electricity grid. You use solar power when available; the grid covers the rest. No battery.
- Off-grid: Your solar system is completely independent of the grid. You store power in batteries. No grid connection at all.
- Hybrid: Your solar system is connected to the grid AND has battery storage. Best of both worlds — but costs more.

## On-Grid Solar System — Deep Dive

### How It Works

Your solar panels generate DC power. The inverter converts it to AC. This AC power runs your home appliances directly. Any excess power is exported to the grid and credited to your electricity bill — this is called net metering.

At night or on cloudy days when your panels aren't generating enough, the grid supplies power normally.

[IMAGE:0]

### Pros of On-Grid Solar

- Lowest cost — no battery needed
- Highest ROI and shortest payback period
- Net metering gives you credits for surplus generation
- Eligible for PM Surya Ghar subsidy
- Virtually zero maintenance
- Best for: Noida, Greater Noida, Ghaziabad, Gurugram (areas with reliable grid)

### Cons of On-Grid Solar

- During a power cut, the system shuts down automatically (for safety reasons)
- No backup power at night unless grid is available

### On-Grid Cost in Noida (2026)

| System Size | Cost Before Subsidy | After Subsidy |
|---|---|---|
| 3 kW | ₹1,95,000 | ₹1,17,000 |
| 4 kW | ₹2,40,000 | ₹1,62,000 |
| 5 kW | ₹3,00,000 | ₹2,22,000 |

### Who Should Choose On-Grid?

If your area has reliable electricity supply (less than 2–3 hours of power cuts per day), on-grid is almost always the right choice. This includes most of Noida, Greater Noida, Ghaziabad, Gurugram, and urban UP cities.

## Off-Grid Solar System — Deep Dive

### How It Works

Off-grid systems are completely disconnected from the utility grid. Your solar panels charge a battery bank during the day. You draw power from the batteries at night or when panels aren't generating. If the batteries are depleted and panels aren't generating, you have no power — period.

### Pros of Off-Grid Solar

- Works in areas with no grid connectivity at all
- Complete energy independence
- Useful for remote farms, agricultural pumps, village applications

### Cons of Off-Grid Solar

- Much higher cost (large battery bank required)
- Not eligible for PM Surya Ghar subsidy
- Battery replacement every 5–8 years adds long-term cost
- Complicated system management
- NOT recommended for urban/semi-urban homes with grid access

### Who Should Choose Off-Grid?

Off-grid is ideal for rural areas, agricultural land, or any location where the electricity grid simply doesn't reach. For urban homeowners in Noida or Gurugram, off-grid makes very little financial sense.

## Hybrid Solar System — Deep Dive

### How It Works

A hybrid system combines an on-grid solar setup with a battery bank. During the day, solar panels power your home and charge the battery. At night, the battery powers your home. If the battery is depleted, the grid takes over. During a power cut, the battery keeps your critical appliances running.

[IMAGE:1]

### Pros of Hybrid Solar

- Backup power during load shedding (power cuts) — critical for many UP homes
- Can store excess solar energy instead of just exporting it
- Works with net metering (in most DISCOM configurations)
- Best of on-grid savings + battery backup

### Cons of Hybrid Solar

- Significantly higher upfront cost (battery adds ₹80,000–₹2,00,000+)
- Battery has a lifespan of 8–12 years (lithium) — replacement cost later
- Adding a battery may reduce or eliminate PM Surya Ghar subsidy eligibility — check with your installer

### Hybrid Solar Cost in Noida (2026)

| System Size | On-Grid Cost | Add Battery (5 kWh) | Total Hybrid Cost |
|---|---|---|---|
| 3 kW | ₹1,95,000 | ₹1,10,000 | ₹3,05,000 |
| 4 kW | ₹2,40,000 | ₹1,10,000 | ₹3,50,000 |
| 5 kW | ₹3,00,000 | ₹1,20,000 | ₹4,20,000 |

### Who Should Choose Hybrid?

Hybrid is the right choice if your area experiences frequent power cuts (4+ hours/day) and you need to run essential appliances like fans, lights, router, and a refrigerator during outages. Many customers in interior UP districts, Ghaziabad industrial areas, and semi-urban Haryana prefer hybrid systems.

## Side-by-Side Comparison

| Feature | On-Grid | Off-Grid | Hybrid |
|---|---|---|---|
| Cost | Low | High | Medium-High |
| Backup During Power Cut | No | Yes | Yes |
| Net Metering | Yes | No | Yes (usually) |
| PM Surya Ghar Subsidy | Yes | No | Partial |
| Best For | Urban homes | Remote/rural | Frequent cuts |
| Payback Period | 3–4 years | 6–9 years | 4–6 years |
| Maintenance | Minimal | High | Low-Medium |

## What Volta Solar Recommends for NCR Homes

For 80% of our customers in Noida, Greater Noida, Ghaziabad, and Gurugram — we recommend on-grid solar. The grid reliability in these areas is high, the subsidy benefit is maximum, and the ROI is fastest.

For customers in areas with 4–8 hour daily power cuts (common in UP towns like Meerut, Bareilly, Moradabad, Agra) — we recommend a hybrid system sized to cover essential loads during outages.

Off-grid is almost never the right answer for anyone with an existing electricity connection.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Can I convert my on-grid system to hybrid later?

Yes. If you install a hybrid-ready inverter from day one, you can add a battery later without replacing the inverter. Volta Solar always offers this 'battery-ready' option during system design. It saves you money if you decide to add backup power in the future.

### Does a hybrid solar system work during a power cut in Noida?

Yes, that's the primary advantage of hybrid over on-grid. During a power cut, the battery automatically supplies power to your home within milliseconds. Most customers run fans, lights, TV, router, and a small refrigerator on a standard 5 kWh battery.

### Is on-grid solar safe if power cuts are frequent?

On-grid solar is perfectly safe but does not provide backup. During a power cut, the inverter disconnects from the grid (an anti-islanding safety feature required by regulation) and your home has no power. If backup is important to you, choose hybrid.

### How many hours of backup does a 5 kWh battery provide?

It depends on your load. For basic essentials (4 fans + 8 LED lights + router), a 5 kWh lithium battery gives approximately 6–8 hours of backup. For a refrigerator running continuously, add another 200–300W to your load calculation.

### Can I get PM Surya Ghar subsidy on a hybrid system?

This depends on your DISCOM and how the system is configured. In most cases, hybrid systems with grid-tied operation qualify for subsidy. However, purely off-grid systems do not. Always confirm with your installer before purchase.

## Not Sure Which System to Choose?

That's exactly why Volta Solar offers free site surveys. Our engineers assess your roof, your electricity bills, your area's power cut frequency, and your budget — then recommend the system that gives you the best returns.

No upselling. No commission-driven recommendations. Just the system that makes the most sense for your home.

📞 Call us: +91 93359 35751

Get your free site survey and a detailed system recommendation within 24 hours.

title: UPPCL Net Metering: Complete Application Process for Noida & Greater Noida (2026)
excerpt: Everything you need to know about UPPCL net metering in Noida, Greater Noida, and Ghaziabad — what it is, why it matters, documents required, timeline, and how Volta Solar handles the full process for you.
author: Volta Solar Expert Team

inlineImages.name: UPPCL electricity meter
inlineImages.caption: A bidirectional net meter installed by PVVNL in Noida — this meter runs forward when you use grid power and backward when you export solar power.

inlineImages.name: Solar net metering benefit calculation
inlineImages.caption: Net metering credits accumulate monthly and are adjusted in your electricity bill — effectively making your solar system earn money while you're not home.

content:
You've installed solar panels. The system is generating clean electricity. But here's the thing — during the middle of the day, your panels are producing far more power than your home is consuming. Where does that excess power go?

Without net metering, it's wasted. With net metering, it's exported to the grid and credited to your electricity bill. This single feature can reduce your annual electricity bill to near-zero — or even result in a net credit.

This guide explains exactly how UPPCL net metering works in Noida, Greater Noida, and Ghaziabad, and how to get it done quickly.

## What Is Net Metering?

Net metering is a billing mechanism that allows solar energy system owners to export excess electricity to the grid and receive credits against their electricity consumption.

Think of the grid as a giant battery. When your solar panels produce more than you need, you 'store' it in the grid. When you need power (at night or on cloudy days), you 'withdraw' from the grid. Your monthly bill reflects only the net difference.

If you export more than you consume in a month, the credit rolls over to the next month.

[IMAGE:0]

## How Net Metering Works in Noida — PVVNL

Noida, Greater Noida, and Ghaziabad fall under PVVNL (Paschim Vitaran Vidyut Nigam Limited), which is part of UPPCL. Here's how the billing works:

- Your existing single-direction meter is replaced with a bidirectional (net) meter
- The meter records both import (grid to home) and export (solar to grid) readings
- Your monthly bill = Units consumed from grid − Units exported to grid
- If exported units > imported units, the surplus is carried forward as a credit
- Credit can be carried forward for up to 12 months (as per UPPCL policy)
- At year-end settlement, any remaining credit may be adjusted at a lower rate

## Net Metering Rates in UP (2026)

The rate at which UPPCL compensates you for exported solar power is called the feed-in tariff or solar energy purchase rate.

| Consumer Category | Import Rate (₹/unit) | Export Credit Rate (₹/unit) |
|---|---|---|
| Domestic (0–100 units) | ₹5.50 | ₹3.00–₹3.50 |
| Domestic (101–200 units) | ₹6.00 | ₹3.00–₹3.50 |
| Domestic (201–300 units) | ₹6.50 | ₹3.00–₹3.50 |
| Commercial | ₹7.00–₹8.50 | ₹3.50–₹4.00 |

Note: Import and export rates are set by UPERC (UP Electricity Regulatory Commission) and are subject to annual revision.

While the export rate is lower than the import rate, smart solar system sizing ensures you consume most of your solar output directly — only exporting the surplus. This maximizes your effective savings.

## Documents Required for PVVNL Net Meter Application

- Completed net metering application form (available on PVVNL / UPPCL portal)
- Copy of latest electricity bill
- Copy of Aadhaar card
- Solar system installation completion certificate (from your installer)
- Single-line diagram of the solar installation
- Technical specifications of solar panels and inverter
- Photographs of the installed system
- No-objection certificate from housing society (if applicable)

Volta Solar prepares and submits all these documents for every customer. You don't fill out a single form.

[IMAGE:1]

## Step-by-Step UPPCL Net Metering Process

### Step 1 — Solar Installation Complete

Before applying for net metering, your solar system must be fully installed and commissioned. This typically takes 2–5 working days for a residential system.

### Step 2 — Submit Net Metering Application

Your installer submits the net metering application to the DISCOM (PVVNL for Noida) through the PM Surya Ghar portal or directly to the divisional office. Volta Solar submits this on the day of installation completion.

### Step 3 — Technical Inspection

The DISCOM sends a technical team to inspect your installation. They verify that the system meets safety standards and is properly installed. This typically happens within 7–21 days of application.

### Step 4 — Net Meter Installation

Once the inspection is passed, the DISCOM replaces your existing meter with a bidirectional net meter. This is done by their team at no charge to you.

### Step 5 — System Goes Live

Once the net meter is installed, your solar system officially starts earning credits. From this day, every unit you export to the grid appears as a credit on your next electricity bill.

### Typical Timeline in Noida (2026)

| Step | Expected Time |
|---|---|
| Application submission | Day 1 (post-installation) |
| Technical inspection | 7–21 days |
| Net meter installation | 15–30 days from application |
| First credited bill | Next billing cycle |
| PM Surya Ghar subsidy | 30–60 days post net meter |

## Common Reasons for Net Metering Delays — and How We Handle Them

Unfortunately, DISCOM delays are common. Here's what causes them and how Volta Solar manages the process:

- Incomplete documentation: We ensure all docs are 100% complete on Day 1
- Inspector not available: We have direct contacts at PVVNL and DHBVN offices and follow up every 5 days
- Society NOC pending: We advise customers on this before installation begins
- Technical query from DISCOM: We respond to queries within 24 hours with proper documentation

On average, Volta Solar customers in Noida get their net meter installed within 25–35 days of application — faster than the DISCOM's stated 30-day SLA.

## How Much Can You Save With Net Metering in Noida?

Let's take a 4 kW system in Sector 100, Noida as an example.

- Monthly solar generation: ~480 units
- Direct self-consumption (during day): ~280 units
- Units exported to grid: ~200 units
- Export credit earned: 200 × ₹3.25 = ₹650/month
- Direct savings (avoided grid purchase): 280 × ₹6.50 = ₹1,820/month
- Total monthly benefit: ₹2,470
- Annual savings: ~₹29,640

Combined with morning and evening self-consumption, the total annual savings for a 4 kW system in Noida is typically ₹55,000–₹70,000.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Is net metering mandatory for solar in UP?

No, it's not mandatory — but it is highly recommended. Without net metering, any excess solar power you generate is wasted. Net metering is what makes solar financially excellent in urban areas like Noida and Ghaziabad.

### Can apartments and housing societies get net metering in Noida?

Individual flat owners with their own electricity connection can apply for net metering for rooftop solar if they have dedicated rooftop access. Common area solar for entire societies requires bulk net metering arrangements — Volta Solar handles both.

### How do I check my net metering credit on my UPPCL bill?

Your net metering credit appears as 'Solar export units' or 'Net energy adjustment' on your monthly PVVNL/UPPCL electricity bill. You can also track it through the UPPCL consumer portal or mobile app.

### What is the maximum solar system size allowed for net metering in UP?

For residential consumers in UP, net metering is allowed for systems up to 500 kW, but practically for homes, systems up to 10 kW are standard. The system capacity must not exceed your sanctioned load from UPPCL.

### Does net metering work during a power cut?

No. During a power cut, on-grid solar systems automatically shut down (anti-islanding protection). Net metering only functions when the grid is live. If backup power during cuts is important, consider adding a battery (hybrid system).

## Let Us Handle Your Net Metering

Net metering is the backbone of your solar investment's returns. Without it, you're leaving significant money on the table.

Volta Solar handles every net metering application in Noida, Greater Noida, Ghaziabad, and Gurugram — from document preparation to DISCOM follow-up to final meter installation.

📞 Call us: +91 93359 35751

Install solar with us and we will not rest until your net meter is active and your credits are flowing.

title: Best Solar Panels in India 2026: Jinko vs Waaree vs Adani — Which Should You Buy?
cardTitle: Jinko vs Waaree vs Adani: Best Solar Panels?
excerpt: Confused about which solar panels to choose for your Noida or Gurugram home? We compare Jinko Solar, Waaree, and Adani Solar on efficiency, price, warranty, and real-world performance — with our honest recommendation.
author: Volta Solar Expert Team

inlineImages.name: Solar panel efficiency comparison
inlineImages.caption: Modern TOPCon and bifacial solar panels from Tier-1 manufacturers deliver 22%+ efficiency — a significant improvement over the 15–17% panels sold just 5 years ago.

inlineImages.name: Waaree solar panels installed India
inlineImages.caption: Waaree Energies — India's largest solar panel manufacturer — produces panels that perform exceptionally in the high-dust, high-temperature conditions of UP and NCR.

content:
You've decided to go solar. You've got quotes from a few installers. And now every quote mentions different panel brands — Jinko, Waaree, Adani, Vikram, REC, Longi.

How do you know which one is actually good? How do you know the installer isn't pushing a brand that gives them higher margins?

This guide gives you the honest truth about the three most commonly installed solar panel brands in Noida and Gurugram — so you can make an informed decision.

## What Makes a Solar Panel 'Good'?

Before comparing brands, understand what metrics actually matter:

- Efficiency: What percentage of sunlight is converted to electricity? Higher = more power from smaller area
- Temperature coefficient: How much does output drop as temperature rises? Critical for UP summers (45°C+)
- Degradation rate: How fast does output decline over 25 years? Lower degradation = more lifetime energy
- Warranty: Linear power warranty (guaranteeing output at year 10, 15, 25) vs basic product warranty
- Tier classification: Bloomberg New Energy Finance rates manufacturers — Tier 1 means bankable, high-quality
- India suitability: High dust tolerance, performance under diffuse light (cloudy/hazy conditions in UP)

## Jinko Solar — The Global Market Leader

Jinko Solar is the world's largest solar panel manufacturer by shipment volume. Founded in China, Jinko has a manufacturing facility in India and sells widely across NCR and UP.

### Key Specifications (2026)

| Specification | Jinko Tiger Neo N-Type |
|---|---|
| Efficiency | 22.3%–23.0% |
| Temperature Coefficient | -0.29%/°C |
| Year 1 Degradation | ≤1% |
| Annual Degradation | ≤0.4% |
| Year 25 Guaranteed Output | 87.4% |
| Product Warranty | 12 years |
| Power Warranty | 25 years linear |
| Tier Classification | Bloomberg Tier 1 |

### Jinko Performance in UP Climate

Jinko's N-Type TOPCon technology performs particularly well in high-temperature environments. The low temperature coefficient (-0.29%/°C) means output drops less on a 45°C Noida summer day compared to older P-Type panels (-0.38%/°C). This matters — on a 45°C day, a panel with -0.29% coefficient loses about 5.5% output vs 7.2% for a -0.38% panel.

### Jinko Price (Noida, 2026)

Jinko Tiger Neo panels cost approximately ₹28,000–₹32,000 per panel (370–420W), inclusive of GST. For a 4 kW system (10 panels), panel cost is ₹2,80,000–₹3,20,000 at the panel level alone.

[IMAGE:0]

## Waaree Energies — India's Largest Solar Manufacturer

Waaree Energies is India's largest solar panel manufacturer, headquartered in Mumbai with large manufacturing facilities in Surat and Chikhli. They are MNRE-approved and among the most popular choices for subsidized residential solar in India.

### Key Specifications (2026)

| Specification | Waaree Mono PERC / TOPCon |
|---|---|
| Efficiency | 20.5%–22.0% |
| Temperature Coefficient | -0.32%/°C |
| Year 1 Degradation | ≤2% |
| Annual Degradation | ≤0.45% |
| Year 25 Guaranteed Output | 84.8% |
| Product Warranty | 10 years |
| Power Warranty | 25 years linear |
| Tier Classification | Bloomberg Tier 1 |

### Why Waaree Is Popular for Residential Solar in UP

Waaree's biggest advantage for Indian customers is domestic manufacturing — panels are made in India, service is faster, and spare parts availability is better. They also have an excellent track record with MNRE and UPPCL approvals, which matters when processing subsidies.

In dusty conditions like Noida and Agra, Waaree's anti-reflective coating performs consistently. Their price point makes them the most popular choice among residential customers.

### Waaree Price (Noida, 2026)

Waaree Mono PERC panels are priced at ₹22,000–₹26,000 per panel (380–415W). They are typically 15–20% less expensive than Jinko N-Type, making a 4 kW system more accessible for first-time buyers.

[IMAGE:1]

## Adani Solar — Fast-Growing With Strong Government Backing

Adani Solar (formerly part of Adani Green Energy) has rapidly become one of India's top solar panel manufacturers. With massive expansion in manufacturing capacity in Mundra, Gujarat, Adani now competes directly with Waaree on both quality and pricing.

### Key Specifications (2026)

| Specification | Adani HiKu6 Mono PERC |
|---|---|
| Efficiency | 20.8%–21.5% |
| Temperature Coefficient | -0.34%/°C |
| Year 1 Degradation | ≤2% |
| Annual Degradation | ≤0.45% |
| Year 25 Guaranteed Output | 84.8% |
| Product Warranty | 10 years |
| Power Warranty | 25 years linear |
| Tier Classification | Bloomberg Tier 1 |

### Adani Solar — Key Strengths

Adani Solar panels are ALMM (Approved List of Models and Manufacturers) listed — which is important for PM Surya Ghar subsidy processing. Only ALMM-listed panels qualify for government-subsidized installations.

For commercial installations (factories, warehouses, schools), Adani Solar has strong institutional relationships and volume pricing that makes them very competitive.

## Head-to-Head Comparison

| Factor | Jinko Tiger Neo | Waaree TOPCon | Adani HiKu6 |
|---|---|---|---|
| Efficiency | Highest (22.3%+) | Good (22.0%) | Good (21.5%) |
| Temperature Performance | Best (-0.29%) | Good (-0.32%) | Average (-0.34%) |
| 25-Year Output Guarantee | 87.4% | 84.8% | 84.8% |
| Price Per Panel | Premium | Mid-range | Mid-range |
| ALMM Listed | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| India Manufacturing | Partial | Full | Full |
| Best For | Max output, premium | Residential subsidy | Commercial / Govt |

## Volta Solar's Honest Recommendation

Here's what we actually tell our customers:

For most residential customers in Noida and Gurugram — Waaree or Adani with a good hybrid inverter gives the best value. The efficiency difference between Jinko N-Type and Waaree PERC matters most when rooftop space is severely limited (under 200 sq ft). On a normal 3–4 BHK terrace, any Tier-1 panel works excellent.

For customers with small rooftops (like apartments with limited terrace access) — Jinko Tiger Neo makes sense. Higher efficiency means more power from less space.

For commercial and industrial installations above 50 kW — we often use Jinko or Adani for their superior warranty terms and output guarantee percentages.

What matters MORE than the brand:

- The inverter quality (Sungrow, Growatt, SolarEdge)
- Proper shadow analysis before installation
- Quality of mounting and wiring
- After-sales support from your installer

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Are Chinese solar panels (like Jinko) reliable for long-term use in India?

Jinko is a Bloomberg Tier-1 manufacturer with over 130 GW of panels shipped globally. They carry the same international warranty in India as everywhere else. The concern about Chinese panels is mostly about unknown unbranded manufacturers — not established Tier-1 brands.

### Do solar panels degrade faster in UP's hot climate?

All solar panels lose some efficiency in extreme heat, but Tier-1 panels are tested to international standards (IEC 61215) that include temperature cycling and thermal stress. Real-world degradation for Tier-1 panels in NCR conditions is typically 0.4–0.5% per year — well within warranty specifications.

### Can I mix different panel brands in one system?

Technically possible but not recommended. Mixing panels with different wattages, Voc, and Isc values in the same string can cause mismatch losses. Volta Solar always installs uniform panels from a single batch on every project.

### Does the panel brand matter for PM Surya Ghar subsidy?

Yes. Only ALMM-listed panels qualify for PM Surya Ghar subsidy. All three brands — Jinko, Waaree, and Adani — are ALMM listed. Volta Solar exclusively uses ALMM-listed panels so your subsidy is never at risk.

### How do I verify a panel's specifications and warranty?

Ask your installer for the datasheet from the manufacturer's official website. Check that the serial numbers on delivered panels match the brand's verification system. Volta Solar provides full panel documentation at handover.

## Get the Right Panels for Your Roof

Panel brand is important, but it's one piece of a larger puzzle. The right system design, the right inverter, and a quality installation matter equally.

Volta Solar designs every system based on your specific rooftop, your energy consumption, and your budget. We'll recommend the exact panel and inverter combination that gives you the best returns — with full transparency on costs.

📞 Call us: +91 93359 35751

Get a free site survey and a system recommendation backed by real data — not commission-driven upselling.

title: Solar Panel Maintenance Guide for UP & NCR Homes: How to Clean & Maximize Output (2026)
cardTitle: Solar Maintenance Guide: Maximize Your Output
excerpt: Your solar panels need very little maintenance — but the right care makes a real difference to output and lifespan. This practical guide covers cleaning frequency, what to check, and how to spot problems early in Noida, Gurugram, and UP.
author: Volta Solar Expert Team

inlineImages.name: Solar panel cleaning in progress
inlineImages.caption: Cleaning solar panels with a soft brush and clean water — the most effective and safest method for dust removal in NCR's high-pollution environment.

inlineImages.name: Solar inverter monitoring display
inlineImages.caption: Modern solar inverters display real-time output data — checking this weekly helps you catch drops in performance before they become costly problems.

content:
One of the best things about solar panels is how little maintenance they need. No fuel. No moving parts. No weekly servicing.

But 'low maintenance' doesn't mean 'zero maintenance.' In Noida, Gurugram, and across Uttar Pradesh, the combination of summer dust storms, monsoon bird droppings, and winter fog means a panel that isn't cleaned properly can lose 15–25% of its output.

This guide gives you a practical, actionable maintenance routine that takes less than 30 minutes per month — and keeps your system running at peak performance for 25+ years.

## Why Solar Panel Maintenance Matters More in UP & NCR

Noida and Gurugram have some of the highest air pollution and dust levels in the world. AQI regularly crosses 300–400 in winter. Summer brings dust storms (aandhi) from Rajasthan. Monsoon brings bird activity and algae growth on panels.

Dust, bird droppings, and soiling are the #1 cause of avoidable solar output loss in North India. A study by NISE (National Institute of Solar Energy) found that un-cleaned solar panels in Delhi-NCR lose an average of 0.5–1% of output per day during high-dust periods.

Over a month without cleaning, that's a 15–25% reduction in generation. On a 4 kW system generating 480 units/month, that's 72–120 units lost — worth ₹470–₹780 in electricity credits.

## How Often Should You Clean Solar Panels in Noida?

| Season | Cleaning Frequency | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| Summer (Apr–Jun) | Every 3–4 weeks | Dust storms, high soiling rate |
| Monsoon (Jul–Sep) | Every 4–6 weeks | Rain helps but bird droppings accumulate |
| Post-monsoon (Oct–Nov) | Every 3–4 weeks | Dry weather, moderate dust |
| Winter (Dec–Feb) | Every 2–3 weeks | Fog, smog deposits, very high AQI |

General rule: If you can visibly see dust or a whitish film on your panels, clean them immediately. Don't wait for the schedule.

[IMAGE:0]

## How to Clean Solar Panels — The Right Way

### What You Need

- Soft-bristle brush or microfiber mop (long-handled for safety)
- Clean water (tap water is fine; avoid hard water if possible)
- Garden hose or bucket
- Optional: mild, pH-neutral soap (no detergents or harsh chemicals)

### Step-by-Step Cleaning Process

1. Clean early morning or late evening — never during the hottest part of the day. Cold water on hot glass can cause micro-cracking.
2. If there's heavy dust, spray water first to loosen it. Don't scrub dry dust — it scratches the anti-reflective coating.
3. Use the soft brush or mop to gently wipe in one direction (top to bottom).
4. Rinse thoroughly with clean water.
5. Let air-dry — do not wipe with rough cloth.

### What NOT to Do

- Never use abrasive cleaners, steel wool, or harsh chemicals
- Never walk on solar panels
- Never clean with high-pressure jets (jet washers) — they can damage junction boxes
- Never use hard/mineralized water repeatedly — mineral deposits reduce transparency over time
- Avoid cleaning in strong wind — debris can scratch panels

## Monsoon Season — Special Considerations

Many customers assume monsoon rains are enough to keep panels clean. They're not.

Rainwater in NCR often contains dissolved pollutants and dust particles that leave residue when they dry. Bird droppings are particularly problematic in monsoon because birds are more active.

Bird droppings cause 'hot spots' — localized areas where one cell in the panel is shaded or soiled, causing the entire string to underperform. If left for more than 2–3 weeks, hot spots can permanently damage cells.

Clean panels every 4–6 weeks during monsoon even if it's been raining.

## Winter Fog — The Silent Output Killer

In Noida and Ghaziabad, December and January bring dense fog. Fog deposits a fine, sticky film on panels that doesn't wash off with rain. Combined with high AQI (400+) during this period, winter is when many solar owners notice a drop in generation without knowing why.

Clean every 2–3 weeks in winter. Morning cleaning (after fog lifts, before peak sun) is ideal.

[IMAGE:1]

## Monthly Solar System Health Checks — What to Look For

Cleaning panels is just one part of maintenance. Here's a simple monthly checklist:

### Inverter Check (5 minutes)

- Check the inverter display for any error codes or warning lights
- Note the daily generation figure — compare to previous weeks of similar weather
- A sudden drop of 10%+ with no change in weather = potential problem

### Panel Visual Check (10 minutes)

- Look for cracks, discoloration, or dark spots on any panel (visible from roof edge)
- Check for birds nesting under the panel array — this is common and can damage wiring
- Look for shadows from new construction, trees, or satellite dishes that may have appeared

### Wiring & Connection Check (5 minutes — once per quarter)

- Look for exposed wires, loose conduit, or any signs of rodent damage
- Check that the earthing wire is secure
- Ensure junction boxes are closed and sealed

### Generation Log (2 minutes)

- Keep a simple monthly log of total units generated
- Compare month-on-month (adjusting for seasonality)
- A consistent decline of more than 5% from the same month last year warrants a service call

## Annual Professional Service — What It Covers

Volta Solar offers Annual Maintenance Contracts (AMC) starting at ₹3,500/year for residential systems. A professional service visit includes:

- Full panel cleaning with professional equipment
- Inverter software update and parameter check
- DC and AC wiring inspection and tightening
- Earthing continuity test
- String performance test (identifies underperforming panels)
- Thermal imaging check (detects hotspots invisible to naked eye)
- Written performance report

For customers without AMC, Volta Solar offers one-time service visits at ₹1,500–₹3,000 depending on system size.

## How Much Does Poor Maintenance Cost You?

Let's quantify it for a 4 kW system in Noida:

| Maintenance Level | Annual Generation | Annual Savings |
|---|---|---|
| Well-maintained (clean + serviced) | 5,760 units | ₹37,440 |
| Cleaned every 3 months only | 5,040 units (−12%) | ₹32,760 |
| Never cleaned | 4,320 units (−25%) | ₹28,080 |

The difference between well-maintained and never-cleaned: ₹9,360 per year. Over 10 years: ₹93,600 in lost savings. That's almost half your original system cost wasted through neglect.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Do solar panels need cleaning in the monsoon season?

Yes. While rain removes some dust, it leaves dissolved pollutant residue. Bird droppings are more frequent in monsoon and cause hot spots. Clean every 4–6 weeks even during the rainy season in UP and NCR.

### Can I clean my solar panels myself or should I hire a professional?

For single-storey or easily accessible rooftops, self-cleaning with a soft brush and water is perfectly fine. For multi-storey buildings, steep rooftops, or large systems (10 kW+), hire a professional with proper safety equipment. Never take rooftop risks.

### How do I know if my solar panels are underperforming?

Check your inverter's generation display daily. If generation drops more than 10–15% compared to similar sunny days in the past (and there's no shade or cloud cover), your panels likely need cleaning or a system check. Volta Solar's AMC customers receive monthly performance reports that flag any anomalies.

### What causes hot spots on solar panels and how do I prevent them?

Hot spots are caused by partial shading or soiling on one cell, forcing current from other cells through the shaded cell — generating heat instead of electricity. Prevention: clean panels regularly (especially bird droppings), ensure no new shadows have appeared, and install bypass diodes (good inverters include these). Annual thermographic inspection can detect hot spots before they damage cells.

### How long do solar panels actually last in North India?

Tier-1 panels from Jinko, Waaree, or Adani are designed for 25+ years. With proper maintenance in NCR/UP conditions, well-maintained systems routinely perform well beyond 25 years. The inverter typically needs replacement at 10–15 years (cost: ₹30,000–₹70,000 depending on size). Panels themselves rarely fail if properly installed.

### Is Volta Solar's AMC worth it?

For most customers, yes. At ₹3,500/year, you get professional cleaning twice a year, a full wiring and safety inspection, and a performance report. One visit of professional cleaning alone prevents ₹5,000–₹9,000 in annual generation losses. Plus you get priority response if anything goes wrong with your system.

## Keep Your Solar Investment Performing at Its Best

Your solar system is a 25-year investment. A little consistent care makes the difference between a system that performs at 95% efficiency year after year — and one that quietly loses output while you assume everything is fine.

Volta Solar's AMC is available for all our existing customers and for systems installed by other companies. We'll assess your system honestly and tell you exactly what it needs.

📞 Call us for an AMC quote: +91 93359 35751

Or book a one-time service visit — we'll inspect, clean, and certify your system's health within 48 hours.
